convention_decisions.decision: Restrict v1 to a single pion two-point correlator at zero momentum.
convention_decisions.why: The local examples separate zero-momentum and dispersion workflows; the external citations justify the narrow two-point zero-momentum convention.

external_citations.chosen_convention: Use the standard proton interpolating operator together with a zero-momentum Euclidean two-point correlator and parity projection, matching the narrow local PyQUDA example.
external_citations.knowledge_boundary_note: This citation record comes from a local curated JSON artifact, not from a live online lookup during the current run.
external_citations.title: Hadron Spectroscopy with Lattice QCD
external_citations.why_needed: The local PyQUDA proton example fixes the implementation path; this curated review record documents the physics-side proton spectroscopy convention without implying live online lookup.

external_lookup.reason: External lookup is disabled by default. Re-run with --enable-external-lookup to allow live online lookup.

preflight_checks: Ensure all gauge inputs are visible from every rank before launch.
Ensure the output directory is writable by the rank that saves the final correlator artifact.
Verify numpy, cupy, pyquda_utils, and pyquda import successfully before starting a full inversion job.
Review the sibling .physics.json, .task.json, and .plan.json artifacts before cluster submission.
handoff_contract.submission_assumption: Launch under an MPI/GPU layout consistent with GRID_SIZE and the target PyQUDA environment.

inferred_interpretation.label: baryon two-point correlator with unresolved channel/operator
inferred_interpretation.summary: The request asks for a baryon correlator, but the baryon channel is not specific enough to map directly to a supported implementation. The current grounded local baryon path is proton two-point only.

live_online_lookup.note: Live online lookup is opt-in and only used when local PyQUDA evidence is insufficient for the physics-side target definition.

local_curated_citations.note: Curated local JSON citations are available and may be attached to the physics artifact or implementation plan.

model_inference.note: Model inference may be used for normalization or formula/operator explanation when an LLM backend is configured.

true_online_lookup.note: Legacy boundary label retained for compatibility. The active opt-in capability is recorded under knowledge_boundary.live_online_lookup.

blockers: No concrete local PyQUDA references were attached to the implementation plan.

blockers.suggestion: Use a GPU-enabled Python environment with CuPy installed and matching CUDA runtime support.
Build or install the PyQUDA core bindings in this Python environment before runtime proof.
Expose the local PyQUDA checkout on PYTHONPATH or install pyquda_utils into the runtime environment.
Check that pyquda_utils is importable and that the built core helpers are present.

generated_script_probe.reason: The run path does not auto-execute generated workflow scripts because they may launch a real PyQUDA inversion workload.

probe_policy.reason: Generated-script probes are opt-in because a complete script may start a real PyQUDA inversion workload.

unsupported_reasons: Physics target is not confirmed yet.
user_request: I want a baryon correlator script but I am not sure about the exact operator
validation_checks: generated code imports real PyQUDA APIs
generated code matches local examples/tests/io helpers for the chosen workflow
generated code contains no TODO/pass/placeholder text
